topic: Sparse memory MVP implementation + assumption audit
key_finding: kNN memory retrieval only validated at 125M-350M scale in literature; 7B scale with 128 slots is 4 orders of magnitude too small relative to Memorizing Transformers ratio (262K entries for 125M models).
recommended_next: Run NIH-Extended on base model first to establish degradation curve, then evaluate sparse memory zero-training on configs where base < 95%. If no benefit even at 8192 slots, consider pivot to external kNN index.

key_finding: Gate failure is architectural: 4000x gradient dilution (per-token gating) + sigmoid saturation (bias=+2.0) + zero-weight init. Not a DDP bug (already fixed). Recommended: Phase 1 test better init, Phase 2 adopt concatenation fusion (proven in Memorizing Transformers).
recommended_next: Run Phase 1 quick test (better init) to validate gradient flow, then implement Phase 2 concatenation fusion as primary solution.
